instruction: Analyze the business data and suggest growth strategies
input: The company has 90 customers. Repeat rate is 45%. Most customers are aged 18-25. Social media engagement is low.
output: The main issues are low customer loyalty and weak online presence. Strategies: 1) Launch a referral program to leverage young customers. 2) Improve social media content using short videos and influencers. 3) Introduce loyalty rewards to increase repeat rate. Expected impact: 30-50% customer growth within 6 months.

instruction: Analyze customer data and provide a growth plan
input: The company has 120 customers, repeat rate is 70%, average age is 35, but new customer acquisition is slow.
output: The company has strong retention but weak acquisition. Strategies: 1) Invest in targeted online ads. 2) Partner with complementary businesses. 3) Offer limited-time promotions for new customers. Expected impact: 25-40% growth.

instruction: Suggest strategies to increase customers by 50%
input: The business has 70 customers, low repeat purchases, and poor website conversion.
output: Key problems are low retention and website inefficiency. Strategies: 1) Redesign website for better user experience. 2) Add customer testimonials. 3) Simplify checkout process. Expected impact: up to 50% increase in customers.

instruction: Evaluate the company and suggest improvements
input: The company has 200 customers, repeat rate is high, but profit margins are shrinking.
output: The main issue is cost control rather than customer volume. Strategies: 1) Optimize supply chain. 2) Increase pricing for premium services. 3) Reduce operational inefficiencies. Expected impact: improved profitability without losing customers.

instruction: Analyze this business and suggest growth opportunities
input: The business has 50 customers, strong social media engagement, but low conversion into paying customers.
output: The problem lies in sales funnel efficiency. Strategies: 1) Add clearer call-to-action. 2) Offer first-time buyer discounts. 3) Improve landing pages. Expected impact: 40-60% increase in paying customers.

instruction: Create a strategy to grow a local business
input: A café has 90 customers per week, mostly students, with low repeat visits.
output: Strategies: 1) Introduce student loyalty cards. 2) Offer bundle discounts. 3) Promote limited-time seasonal drinks. Expected impact: 20-40% weekly customer growth.

input: The company has 150 customers, but customer satisfaction is low and complaints are increasing.
output: The core issue is service quality. Strategies: 1) Improve customer support training. 2) Collect and act on feedback. 3) Introduce service guarantees. Expected impact: improved retention and organic growth.

instruction: Analyze the customer base and suggest expansion strategies
input: The business operates in a small town with 80 customers and limited local reach.
output: The growth bottleneck is geographic reach. Strategies: 1) Launch online sales. 2) Offer delivery services. 3) Use regional digital marketing. Expected impact: 50%+ expansion potential.

instruction: Suggest strategies to improve customer acquisition
input: The company has 110 customers but relies only on word-of-mouth marketing.
output: The company lacks scalable marketing. Strategies: 1) Create a social media marketing plan. 2) Launch referral incentives. 3) Use basic online ads. Expected impact: 30-50% customer increase.

instruction: Evaluate and improve the company performance
input: The business has stable customers but declining revenue per customer.
output: The issue is low average order value. Strategies: 1) Upsell premium options. 2) Bundle products. 3) Offer subscriptions. Expected impact: increased revenue without increasing customer count.
